secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with security/infrastructure domain focus

level_signals: Drives cross-functional RFCs from proposal to shipped code
Expected to define strategy across auth, IAM, compliance, and AI agent security

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 7+ years in product management, with serious time on security, identity and access, infrastructure, or developer platform products at a company where security mattered to enterprise buyers.
must_haves.qualifier: with serious time on security, identity and access, infrastructure, or developer platform products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have deep working knowledge of the security primitives our customers use like authentication, authorization (RBAC, RLS), audit logging, secrets management, OAuth.
Have a track record of leading cross-functional initiatives across Product, Engineering, Security, GTM, and Compliance, and driving multi-team RFCs from proposal to shipped code.
Are 100% comfortable in a remote, async, write-it-down culture.
Are an exceptional writer. You can draft a customer-facing security disclosure, an internal threat model, a docs page, or a one-pager for a CISO without losing voice or precision.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Compliance fluency. You've worked alongside auditors and security teams on programs like SOC2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, PCI, or FedRAMP, and you can tell which requirements are real customer needs and which are checkbox theater.
Technical depth in Postgres, auth systems, or networking primitives.
Experience designing access models for AI agents or other automated systems.
Shipped security features that enterprise CISOs had to approve before adoption.

scope.surface: Platform-wide security and trust: firewall, security advisors, audit logs, Supabase Vault, just-in-time database access, IAM, SSO, SCIM, OAuth integrations, AI agent authentication and scoping, compliance roadmap
scope.business_model: developer platform, B2D and enterprise B2B
primary_metrics_inferred: Security roadmap delivery (SOC2, HIPAA, and future compliance programs)
Developer experience scores vs. security control friction
AI agent authentication and audit coverage
core_responsibilities: Lead Supabase's platform security roadmap end-to-end
Balance security controls against developer experience at platform scale
Lead security strategy for AI agents (authentication, scoping, auditing agent activity)
Own roadmap for security tooling: firewall, security advisors, audit logs, Supabase Vault, just-in-time database access, IAM primitives
Define the unified access model across Supabase (roles, permissions, PATs, OAuth, SSO, SCIM)
Drive compliance roadmap beyond SOC2 and HIPAA
Be the customer's voice for security across enterprise and regulated customers

green_flags: Fully remote with open global hiring across EMEA and AMER
Clear product surface and ownership described in detail
Strong company traction: 7M+ developers, $500M raised, 280+ team members
ESOP equity for all team members
100% health insurance covered for employees, 80% for dependents
Annual company offsite and professional development allowance included
red_flags: 7+ year PM experience bar with domain-specific security knowledge is a narrow candidate pool
Role spans a very broad surface area (IAM, compliance, AI agents, audit, firewall) which could indicate under-resourcing or unclear scope boundaries

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and strategic scope described)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no management language in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description as B2D and enterprise B2B)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.work_auth (inferred as open based on globally distributed hiring language)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred as false from fully-remote global hiring posture)
ambiguities: JD says 'This is a remote position and we're open to considering candidates located across EMEA and AMER time zones' — it is unclear whether candidates outside EMEA/AMER are excluded or merely deprioritized. Treated as remote-global with EMEA/AMER preference.

The must-have requirement for 7+ years mentions 'security, identity and access, infrastructure, or developer platform products' — the 'or' structure means the full domain list is not required, only one qualifying area. Treated as hard given unconditional '7+' framing.
